commands.repair_reason: - [P1] The remaining blocker is contributor/current-head real behavior proof plus maintainer session-state review, not a safe repair-lane task.; Cleared: The diff is limited to TypeScript transcript rotation logic, tests, and a local proof helper, with no dependency, workflow, secret, installer, package-resolution, or supply-chain surface change. (sha=f4f439613ebdcd974689992a322ecad53de147be)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes successor transcript rotation to preserve the assistant reply before the compaction kept boundary, rewrites the successor compaction boundary, adds regression coverage, and adds a one-off proof script.

PR surface: Source +25, Tests +168. Total +193 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main removes summarized message entries and buildSessionContext replays only from firstKeptEntryId after the compaction summary. I did not run a live channel compaction flow in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining blocker is contributor/current-head real behavior proof plus maintainer session-state review, not a safe repair-lane task.

target.title: fix(agents): preserve last assistant reply before compaction boundary (#76729)
target.body:
## Summary

The successor transcript omits the last assistant reply immediately before `firstKeptEntryId`, causing the rotated session to show consecutive user messages without an intermediate reply. For Feishu/WebChat users, this manifests as assistant replies "disappearing" after compaction rotation.

## Changes

- **`buildSuccessorEntries`**: Scan backwards from `firstKeptEntryId`, skip non-context entries (`model_change`, `thinking_level_change`, `session_info`, `custom`, `label`), and adjust `effectiveFirstKeptId` to the last assistant message when found. This ensures the assistant is placed in `preCompactionKeptBranchIds` instead of `summarizedBranchIds`.
- **Successor compaction entry**: Write a modified compaction entry with the adjusted `firstKeptEntryId` so that `buildSessionContext` includes the preserved assistant when replaying the context.
- **Regression test**: New fixture with user-assistant pair + non-context entries before `firstKeptEntryId`, verifying the assistant survives rotation and the summarized user message does not.

## Verification

### Unit tests (12/12 pass)

```
✓ rotateTranscriptAfterCompaction › can rotate a persisted transcript without opening a manager
✓ rotateTranscriptAfterCompaction › creates a compacted successor transcript and leaves the archive untouched
✓ rotateTranscriptAfterCompaction › rotates with a fallback timestamp when the injected clock is invalid
✓ rotateTranscriptAfterCompaction › deduplicates stale pre-compaction session state
✓ rotateTranscriptAfterCompaction › drops duplicate user messages from the rotated active branch tail
✓ rotateTranscriptAfterCompaction › skips sessions with no compaction entry
✓ rotateTranscriptAfterCompaction › uses a refreshed manager after manual boundary hardening
✓ rotateTranscriptAfterCompaction › preserves unsummarized sibling branches and branch summaries
✓ rotateTranscriptAfterCompaction › orders preserved sibling branches after their surviving parents
✓ rotateTranscriptAfterCompaction — assistant boundary preservation › preserves the last assistant message before firstKeptEntryId
✓ shouldRotateCompactionTranscript › keeps transcript rotation opt-in behind the existing config key
✓ rotateTranscriptAfterCompaction — thinking signature stripping › strips thinkingSignature from kept assistant messages in the successor file
```

### Whitespace check
`git diff --check`: clean (no output)

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Successor transcript now preserves the assistant reply immediately before the surviving user message, maintaining `summary → assistant → user → ...` turn structure instead of dropping the assistant.

Real environment tested: Local openclaw checkout at commit `47aad62` on Windows with Node v24.16.0 and pnpm 11.2.2.

Exact commands run after this patch:

```
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/compaction-successor-transcript.test.ts
git diff --check
```

Evidence after fix: All 12 tests pass. The existing `createCompactedSession` fixture now produces:

```json
{"role":"compactionSummary","summary":"Summary of old user and old assistant.",...}
{"role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"text","text":"old assistant"}],"timestamp":2}
{"role":"user","content":"kept user","timestamp":3}
```

The new regression test explicitly verifies:

```json
{"role":"compactionSummary","summary":"Summary of old work.",...}
{"role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"text","text":"summarized answer"}],"timestamp":2}
{"role":"user","content":"surviving question","timestamp":3}
{"role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"text","text":"surviving answer"}],...}
```

What was not tested: A live Control UI/WebChat session with end-to-end compaction. The proof uses the local embedded-agent successor-transcript runtime harness.

## Root cause

In `buildSuccessorEntries`, every `message` entry whose ID is in `summarizedBranchIds` (i.e., before `firstKeptEntryId`) is unconditionally added to `removedIds`. This includes the last assistant reply, which should survive because it provides the immediate conversational context for the first kept user message.

## Proof limitations

L2 proof (local harness replay). A full end-to-end Control UI compaction session was not performed.

## Related

- Previous attempt: #90641 (closed unmerged — boundary discovery bug with non-context entries)
- Previous attempt: #76485 (closed unmerged — mixed preflight queue fix with compaction fix)
- Duplicate-user fix: #93732 (merged, changed dedup scope in same function)
target.author: liuwqgit
